sections.paragraph:
θεοδρόμος, ου, ὁ God’s runner (s. θεός, δρόμος; prob. a new word formed by Ignatius; he may have based it on pass. like Gal 5:7; 1 Cor 9:24–26; 2 Ti 4:7) IPhld 2:2; IPol 7:2.—Cp. DELG s.v. δραμεῖν.

prob. prob. = probable, probably

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

IPhld IPhld = Ignatius to the Philadelphians—List 1

IPol IPol = Ignatius to Polycarp—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 449
